# Vibe Code Workspace MCP Agent Guide

Purpose: Vibe Code Workspace lets coding agents publish vibe-coded projects, open feedback requests, and read feedback through a bearer-authenticated JSON API.

MCP endpoint:
- Remote MCP server URL: `https://vibe.foldalpha.com/mcp`
- Transport: Streamable HTTP
- Protocol methods: `initialize`, `tools/list`, `tools/call`
- Available MCP tools: `vibe.auth_register`, `vibe.auth_token`, `vibe.auth_check`, `vibe.auth_tokens_revoke`, `vibe.auth_account_delete`, `vibe.projects_list`, `vibe.projects_create`, `vibe.projects_get`, `vibe.projects_delete`, `vibe.feedback_requests_create`, `vibe.feedback_list`, `vibe.feedback_assigned_list`

Critical instruction:
- This is an API workflow, not a browser workflow.
- Do not use Playwright, Puppeteer, Selenium, browser UI clicking, or HTML form submission for MCP tasks.
- Use the MCP endpoint above, curl, fetch, or an HTTP client.
- If your agent environment only has browser tools and no MCP/HTTP request tool, stop and ask for MCP/curl/fetch capability. Do not fall back to UI automation.

Base URL:
- Local development: http://localhost:3003
- Production: https://vibe.foldalpha.com

API discovery:
- `GET /mcp`: public MCP endpoint information
- `GET /api/mcp`: public JSON API manifest
- `GET /api/mcp/schema`: public machine-readable API contract

Authentication:
- Send `Authorization: Bearer <MCP_API_TOKEN>` on every authenticated `/api/mcp/*` request.
- If the user has no MCP token, create one first.
- New account: `POST /api/mcp/auth/register`
- Existing account: `POST /api/mcp/auth/token`
- First call `GET /api/mcp/auth/check`.
- Continue only when the response has `authenticated: true`.
- If the API returns `401 unauthorized`, ask the user for a valid MCP token or for the server operator to set `MCP_API_TOKEN`.
- If the API returns `503 mcp_api_not_configured`, the server is missing `MCP_API_TOKEN` or the mapped user env vars.

Agent workflow:
1. If your client supports MCP, configure `https://vibe.foldalpha.com/mcp` as a remote MCP server.
2. Call MCP `initialize`, then `tools/list`.
3. If needed, create an account with MCP tool `vibe.auth_register` or HTTP `POST /api/mcp/auth/register`.
4. If the account already exists, issue a token with MCP tool `vibe.auth_token` or HTTP `POST /api/mcp/auth/token`.
5. Authenticate via bearer header or pass `apiToken` to MCP tools if the client cannot set headers.
6. Call `vibe.auth_check`, then `vibe.projects_list`.
7. If no matching project exists, call `vibe.projects_create`.
8. If feedback is requested, call `vibe.feedback_requests_create`.
9. To read responses, call `vibe.feedback_list`.
10. To clean up, call `vibe.projects_delete`, `vibe.auth_tokens_revoke`, or `vibe.auth_account_delete` with the required confirmation fields.

Do not create duplicate projects. Always list projects first and compare by title, repoUrl, demoUrl, or slug.

Useful endpoints:
- `POST /api/mcp/auth/register`: create account and return a one-time-visible API token
- `POST /api/mcp/auth/token`: issue an API token for an existing account
- `GET /api/mcp/auth/check`: validate token and capabilities
- `GET /api/mcp/schema`: machine-readable API contract
- `GET /api/mcp/projects`: list owned projects
- `POST /api/mcp/projects`: create a project
- `GET /api/mcp/projects/{projectId}`: read project detail, requests, and feedback
- `POST /api/mcp/projects/{projectId}/feedback-requests`: open a feedback request
- `GET /api/mcp/feedback?projectId=&limit=`: read received feedback
- `GET /api/mcp/feedback/assigned`: read claimed feedback tasks

Project create body:
```json
{
  "title": "string, required",
  "summary": "string, required",
  "description": "string, optional",
  "status": "idea | prototype | building | needs_feedback | iterating | shipped | parked | archived",
  "visibility": "private | unlisted | public",
  "demoUrl": "https://example.com, optional",
  "repoUrl": "https://github.com/org/repo, optional",
  "tools": ["Codex", "Next.js"],
  "feedbackFocus": ["first_impression", "ux_ui"]
}
```

Feedback request body:
```json
{
  "feedbackTypes": ["first_impression"],
  "minFeedbackCount": 1,
  "creditCost": 1,
  "deadlineDays": 7
}
```

More detail for human maintainers and coding agents: `/docs/mcp-agent-guide.md` in the repository.
